data.title: Chewy: Strategic Expansion into High-Margin Veterinary Services and Share Buybacks Underpin Buy Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Dylan Carden has maintained their bullish stance on CHWY stock, giving a Buy rating today.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Dylan Carden has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to Chewy’s strategic expansion and capital deployment. He views the Modern Animal acquisition as a meaningful step in building out Chewy’s veterinary services footprint, adding a sizable clinic base, high-margin potential, and a large member cohort, many of whom represent new customers to the Chewy ecosystem.

He also highlights that Modern Animal’s clinics appear to operate at productivity levels above typical industry benchmarks, with a model that shifts lower-value visits to virtual care to maximize in-clinic economics, which should improve further as tech and marketing investments scale under Chewy’s ownership. In addition, he points to Chewy’s enlarged $750 million share repurchase authorization—roughly 7% of its market value—as a signal of management’s confidence and disciplined capital allocation, reinforcing his positive stance despite uncertainties around deal valuation and timing of earnings contribution.

In another report released today, Wolfe Research also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $39.00 price target.

data.title: Chewy’s Acquisition of Modern Animal: Accelerating Growth, Expanding Vet Services, and Enhancing Profitability to Support a Buy Rating
data.description:
Shweta Khajuria, an analyst from Wolfe Research, maintained the Buy rating on Chewy. The associated price target is $39.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Shweta Khajuria has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Chewy’s acquisition of Modern Animal, which meaningfully enhances Chewy’s growth and profitability outlook. The deal is expected to accelerate revenue through stronger demand, incremental customers, and higher spend per active customer, with a clear path toward a materially larger vet-services revenue base by 2027.

Khajuria also highlights the attractive margin potential from operating synergies, premium pricing, and Modern Animal’s above-industry monetization, supporting a favorable long-term EBITDA profile. In addition, disciplined capital allocation, stable CapEx intensity, and continued share repurchases reinforce the view that Chewy can both invest for growth and return capital to shareholders, strengthening the case for a Buy rating on CHWY.

In another report released yesterday, Piper Sandler also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $48.00 price target.

data.title: Validating iBio’s Antibody Platform: Buy Rating Driven by IBIO-600’s Differentiated Obesity Profile and Long-Acting Myostatin Inhibition
data.description:
LifeSci Capital analyst Patrick Dolezal maintained a Buy rating on Ibio today and set a price target of $5.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Patrick Dolezal has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to IBIO-600’s clinical and mechanistic profile. He highlights that IBIO-600’s long half-life and robust myostatin inhibition in obese non-human primates translated into meaningful reductions in fat mass and gains in lean mass, suggesting differentiated potential in obesity treatment, especially alongside incretin drugs to preserve muscle.

He also emphasizes that IBIO-600 serves as an important validation point for iBio’s broader antibody engineering platform, with potential read-through to other assets such as IBIO-610 and the myostatin x activin A bispecific candidate. The shared half-life extension technology across these programs could translate into infrequent dosing in humans, expanding commercial appeal, while the company’s current cash runway supports advancing these assets through key early milestones, collectively underpinning his positive stance on the stock.

According to TipRanks, Dolezal is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 15.9% and a 44.32% success rate. Dolezal covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Ibio, Kodiak Sciences, and Neurogene.

data.title: Balanced Risk-Reward Amid Promising Lunsekimig Data and Valuation Uncertainty Supports Hold Rating
data.description:
Analyst Steve Scala of TD Cowen maintained a Hold rating on Sanofi, retaining the price target of $57.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Steve Scala has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, balancing encouraging drug data with valuation and competitive risks. He views lunsekimig’s Phase II results in asthma and nasal polyps as meaningfully positive, especially the reduction in exacerbations and lung function gains across biomarker subgroups, which reinforce Sanofi’s positioning in a large and growing respiratory biologics market.

At the same time, Scala is cautious because management only characterizes lunsekimig’s profile as “potentially” differentiated versus existing TSLP therapies, and key details such as final dosing strategy and head‑to‑head competitiveness remain uncertain. With expectations already reflecting a sizable opportunity and only modest model adjustments ahead of Q1, he judges the risk‑reward as balanced rather than compelling, supporting a Hold rather than a more aggressive rating.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Crocs: Strengthening Brand Momentum and Margin Upside Support Buy Rating and Higher FY26 Outlook
data.description:
Williams Trading analyst Sam Poser upgraded the rating on Crocs to a Buy today, setting a price target of $116.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sam Poser has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including improving fundamentals and a more favorable outlook for both the Crocs and HEYDUDE brands. He now anticipates a smaller decline in U.S. revenue in FY26 than previously forecast and expects gross margins to exceed company guidance, supported by lower tariffs and strong consumer response to product updates and collaborations.

Sam Poser’s rating is based on evidence that brand momentum is strengthening, with better full‑price sell‑through, especially in sandals and key partnerships, and early signs of recovery at HEYDUDE as it refocuses on its core male, country‑lifestyle customer. In addition, he sees positive shifts in retailer sentiment, more disciplined marketplace and store expansion strategies—particularly in the U.S. outlets and Asian full‑price stores—and believes these trends position Crocs to raise FY26 guidance and support a higher price target.

In another report released on April 6, TipRanks – PerPlexity also upgraded the stock to a Buy with a $95.00 price target.

data.title: Gilead Sciences: Strategic Oncology Expansion and Undervalued Growth Opportunity Driving Buy Rating
data.description:
Gilead Sciences, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Evan Seigerman from BMO Capital maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $174.00 price target.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Evan Seigerman has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Gilead’s expanding growth drivers and attractive valuation. He points to the planned acquisition of Tubulis as a strategically important move that deepens Gilead’s position in oncology and inflammatory diseases, while also creating synergies with the company’s existing antibody-drug conjugate portfolio.

Seigerman also highlights Tubulis’ lead program, TUB-040, which has shown encouraging efficacy and tolerability in platinum-resistant ovarian cancer and is moving toward Phase 3 trials, supporting a more durable oncology franchise beyond HIV. Combined with recent deals for Ouro and Arcellx and the ongoing momentum from key assets like Trodelvy and cell therapies, he believes Gilead is well placed for its next leg of growth, and that the stock’s discount to large-cap peers offers compelling upside as the oncology pipeline is further de-risked.

Seigerman covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Novo Nordisk, Vertex Pharmaceuticals, and Eli Lilly & Co. According to TipRanks, Seigerman has an average return of 8.3% and a 52.24% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Needham also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $170.00 price target.
